title: Psychotria coeloneura
taxonomy: Psychotria coeloneura is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It belongs to the genus Psychotria, which includes over 1,800 species of tropical plants. Psychotria coeloneura is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows in the understory of rainforests. The plant is characterized by its dark green leaves and small, fragrant flowers that are typically white or yellow in color. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Psychotria coeloneura is closely related to other plants with similar characteristics, such as gardenias and jasmine.
